title: testkit: add self-tests for Harness/Mock*/RuntimeHarness
description: pkg/testkit is at 0% coverage with zero *_test.go. It is shared infra used by every module's tests; a bug here silently corrupts all consumers' results. Add unit tests (no infra) for Harness WithData/WithKoanf, WithProvider, ReloadNotifier.FireReload, and the Mock modules incl. RuntimeHarness shutdown timing.
acceptance_criteria: testkit has unit tests covering Harness providers, ReloadNotifier, and mock module behaviors.
close_reason: Added testkit_test.go covering Harness/mocks/RuntimeHarness/WaitForAddr; 0% -> 89% coverage; lint clean.

title: gRPC: test panic-recovery interceptor with a panicking handler
description: pkg/grpc/server/module.go wires recovery.Unary/StreamServerInterceptor but no test invokes a panicking handler. A regression dropping recovery or mis-mapping codes ships green. Register a service whose handler panics; assert client gets a gRPC error (e.g. codes.Unknown) not a dropped conn.
acceptance_criteria: Test calls a panicking handler and asserts the client receives a proper gRPC status, not a connection drop.
close_reason: Added TestGRPCServerModule_PanicRecovery; panicking handler -> codes.Unknown verified. Non-race suite + lint clean; race deferred to end-of-branch sweep.

title: Runtime: test real SIGTERM/SIGINT graceful-shutdown path (Run)
description: pkg/lakta/runtime.go Run() (signal.NotifyContext) is 0%; tests cancel only via the harness, never the signal-driven branch production uses. Risk: SIGTERM handling untested end-to-end.
acceptance_criteria: Test exercises the signal-driven shutdown branch (or its ctx.Done() select arm) and asserts graceful teardown.
close_reason: Added TestRun_SIGTERMTriggersGracefulShutdown (serial, real syscall.Kill SIGTERM); full pkg/lakta suite passes -race + lint.

title: Runtime: test HotReloadable/ValidatableModule wiring to ReloadNotifier
description: pkg/lakta/runtime.go (~91-99): OnReload/OnValidate registration is 0% covered. No test proves a HotReloadable module's callback is wired to ReloadNotifier, nor that OnReload fires on reload. CLAUDE.md advertises hot-reload; it could silently no-op. Unit-testable via testkit FireReload.
acceptance_criteria: Test registers a HotReloadable module, fires a reload, asserts OnReload is invoked with new config.
close_reason: Added TestRunContext_WiresHotReloadAndValidate with a recording notifier; verifies OnReload+ValidateReload registration and callback delivery. Passes -race + lint.

title: Runtime: test async StartAsync failure -> cancel siblings + shutdown
description: pkg/lakta/runtime.go RunContext: when AsyncModule.StartAsync fails, runtime must cancel siblings, run shutdown(), and surface the error. Mock fields (StartAsyncErr/OnStartAsync) exist but NO test drives them. Risk: a failing background task leaves started modules un-shut-down or hangs shutdown. Pure-Go, no infra.
acceptance_criteria: Test drives StartAsync error and asserts siblings are shut down and the error is surfaced.

title: Runtime: test async StartAsync failure -> cancel siblings + shutdown
description: pkg/lakta/runtime.go RunContext: when AsyncModule.StartAsync fails, runtime must cancel siblings, run shutdown(), and surface the error. Mock fields (StartAsyncErr/OnStartAsync) exist but NO test drives them. Risk: a failing background task leaves started modules un-shut-down or hangs shutdown. Pure-Go, no infra.

title: CI: run DB/Temporal integration tests with Docker services
description: ROOT CAUSE: ci-all (mise.toml) runs 'go build && go test && go test -race' per module with no LAKTA_INTEGRATION env and no Docker, so all integration tests skip — green-by-skip. pgx/db.sql gate on LAKTA_INTEGRATION=1; temporal uses //go:build integration. WHAT: add a CI job that starts Postgres (+ Temporal dev server) and runs the suite with LAKTA_INTEGRATION=1 and -tags integration. Unblocks verification of pool create/shutdown, Temporal worker registration, DI registration, and lets -race see the concurrency-heavy paths.
acceptance_criteria: A CI job runs pkg/db/** and pkg/workflows/temporal integration tests against real services; failures there fail CI.
close_reason: Added test-integration mise task + GitHub integration job (Postgres service + Docker testcontainers + temporal dev server, with -race); verified all three suites pass locally.

title: grpc server Shutdown deadlocks calling Stop() concurrently with GracefulStop()
description: Module.Shutdown runs GracefulStop() in a goroutine and calls Stop() on deadline. gRPC funnels both into one stop() sharing a mutex/WaitGroup; concurrent calls deadlock (grpc/grpc-go#8480, #4584). Flaky CI hang in TestShutdown_ForcesStopOnDeadline. Fix: cancel in-flight handler contexts on deadline so the single GracefulStop drains; never call Stop() concurrently.
close_reason: Fixed: Shutdown cancels in-flight handler contexts on deadline instead of calling Stop() concurrently with GracefulStop(); verified green under test-race x20

title: Add fuzz tests for config/koanf parsing and DSN redaction
description: No func Fuzz exists anywhere. Config/koanf parsing and the recently-hardened DSN redaction (commit 7f2e63a) are prime fuzz candidates for panics/leaks on malformed input.
acceptance_criteria: At least one Go fuzz target exists for config parsing and DSN handling; runs clean in CI fuzz mode.
close_reason: Added fuzz targets for parseFlag/envKeyTransform/NewPoolConfig. DSN fuzzer found a real panic (pgxpool.ParseConfig on '=\\') — fixed with a recover in NewPoolConfig + committed crasher as regression seed. Lint clean.

title: Tooling: add tests for cmd/doccheck and cmd/docgen (doc-drift guards)
description: cmd/doccheck (0%) and cmd/docgen (0%) have zero test files. These guard docs-drift in CI; a broken guard passes CI silently. apicheck/genmodules each have one internal test — bring doccheck/docgen to parity.
acceptance_criteria: doccheck and docgen have unit tests verifying drift detection / generation output.
close_reason: Unit tests added by parallel subagents; verified go test + golangci-lint clean. temporal 0->62%, pgx key funcs 100%, doccheck 0->62%, docgen 0->34%.

title: pgx: unit-test ParseLogLevel table, logger.Log mapping, pool sizing, errorType SQLSTATE, LoadConfig

acceptance_criteria: Unit tests cover the full log-level table, logger mapping, pool sizing assertions, SQLSTATE error typing, and koanf load.
close_reason: Unit tests added by parallel subagents; verified go test + golangci-lint clean. temporal 0->62%, pgx key funcs 100%, doccheck 0->62%, docgen 0->34%.

title: Temporal: unit-test config transforms (DialOptions/ClientOptions/WorkerOptions/GetCredentials/LoadFromKoanf)
description: pkg/workflows/temporal: these are pure config->options transforms at 0% (TLS vs insecure, namespace, task queue, koanf decode). No Temporal server needed — highest-value cheap win to lift the 27% number on real logic.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests assert TLS vs insecure options, namespace/task-queue wiring, and koanf decoding.
close_reason: Unit tests added by parallel subagents; verified go test + golangci-lint clean. temporal 0->62%, pgx key funcs 100%, doccheck 0->62%, docgen 0->34%.

title: Servers: test Start bind-failure (port already in use) for grpc + fiber
description: pkg/grpc/server/module.go and pkg/http/fiber/module.go only ever use port 0. The Listen error path (port in use / bad addr) — a common production startup failure — is untested in both.
acceptance_criteria: Tests bind a port, then start a second server on it and assert Start returns a bind error.
close_reason: Added StartBindFailure tests for grpc + fiber (occupy port, assert 'failed to listen'). Lint clean.

title: Shutdown deadline-exceeded: runtime teardown/shutdown + fiber Shutdown
description: pkg/lakta/runtime.go teardown/shutdown 30s-timeout-skip branches (66-69%) are uncovered; a module whose Shutdown hangs past the deadline -> skip rest + return timeout error is unverified at runtime level. pkg/http/fiber module.go Shutdown deadline-exceeded (in-flight requests exceed ctx deadline) untested — grpc's force-Stop equivalent IS tested (asymmetry).
acceptance_criteria: Tests assert deadline-exceeded shutdown skips remaining modules and returns a timeout error; fiber deadline path covered.
close_reason: Added Runtime.shutdown deadline-skip test + fiber ShutdownDeadlineExceeded test (stable x3). teardown's detached-30s branch left untested (would require refactoring prod code; logic mirrors shutdown). Suites + lint clean.

title: otel: test real-exporter init + partial-failure rollback (shutdownAll)
description: pkg/otel module.go Init (46%) + setup.go setupOTelSDK: every test uses SetupFn/disabled. The real provider-build, lakta.ProvideValue DI registration, and exporter-init-failure -> shutdownAll rollback branches never run. Exporter init-failure rollback and DI registration are production-critical.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the real provider build path, DI ProvideValue, and rollback when an exporter init fails.
close_reason: Added rollback test (metric exporter fails on bad endpoint -> trace provider shut down) + DI-registration test for real providers. otel 83%->89%, lint clean.

title: Production-readiness test coverage hardening
description: Close test gaps found in the 2026-06-16 coverage analysis. Suite is healthy on pure-Go logic (9 pkgs >82%, TLS well-covered in pkg/config/tls_test.go) but has critical gaps: DB/Temporal integration tests are green-by-skip in CI, and several core-runtime failure paths are unverified. This epic groups the P0/P1/P2 follow-ups.
close_reason: All 14 child issues complete: CI integration job + 13 test-gap closures across runtime, grpc, otel, fiber, temporal, pgx, testkit, health, config, doc-guards; plus a DSN-panic fix found by fuzzing.

title: Add feature flags module with hot-reload and percentage rollouts (pkg/features/flags)
description: New module: config-only feature flags leveraging existing fsnotify hot-reload. Typed getters + stable-hash percentage rollouts, no external service. Full spec in design field.
design: API: *flags.Flags in DI with Bool/String/Int/Float/Duration(ctx, name, def) + BoolFor(ctx, name, key, def) for rollouts. Config path modules.features.flags.default.flags.<name>; scalar-or-object YAML: scalar for plain values, object {value, rollout} for percentage rollouts. Flag names snake_case (env override via LAKTA_MODULES__FEATURES__FLAGS__DEFAULT__FLAGS__NEW_CHECKOUT; hyphens impossible in env vars, single _ survives envKeyTransform, __ is separator). Snapshot in atomic.Pointer, swapped wholesale in OnReload (module implements lakta.HotReloadable); parse failure keeps old snapshot + logs (matches config.Bind semantics). Rollout: FNV-1a 64 hash of flagName+":"+key % 100 < rollout; flag name in hash decorrelates concurrent rollouts; raising percent is monotonic (only adds users). Bool() on rollout flag returns bare value. Missing flag/type mismatch -> caller default + slox debug. v1 excludes string variants/AB weights and rule targeting (API leaves room). New go.mod + go.work entry.
acceptance_criteria: Typed getters, BoolFor bucketing (deterministic across restarts), hot-reload swap via testkit FireReload, parse-failure-keeps-old all covered by testza tests incl. -race; lint clean; docgen regenerated

title: Add in-process typed event bus module (pkg/events/bus)
description: New module: in-process pub/sub between modules, answering the Pub/Sub idea in IDEAS.md. Type-as-topic generics (Subscribe[T]/Publish), hybrid sync+async per-subscription delivery, bounded queues with publisher backpressure, graceful drain on shutdown. Full spec in design field.
design: API: free generic funcs since Go lacks generic methods: events.Subscribe[T](bus, fn) (sync, errors joined into Publish return), events.SubscribeAsync[T](bus, fn) (per-sub goroutine + bounded chan, FIFO), events.Publish[T](ctx, bus, ev). Single handler sig func(ctx, T) error; panics recovered (sync->error, async->slox log). Async handler ctx = context.WithoutCancel(publisherCtx) so trace/DI values survive request cancellation. Backpressure: bounded queue (config buffer_size, default 1024), full queue blocks Publish (select on ctx.Done). Shutdown: close intake (Publish -> ErrBusClosed), drain queues within runtime deadline. Subscribe returns unsubscribe func(). Module: plain lakta.Module, Provides *events.Bus; config path modules.events.bus.default; NamedBase for multi-instance. Gotchas: value-type events only (T vs *T are distinct topics); sync handler publishing own event type = recursion; subscribers must declare Dependencies() on *events.Bus. New go.mod + go.work entry. Tests: testkit harness + mise run test-race.
acceptance_criteria: Bus module builds as own go.work module; sync/async delivery, backpressure blocking, drain-on-shutdown, panic recovery, unsubscribe all covered by testza tests incl. -race; lint clean via mise; docgen regenerated

title: Assert health failing/degraded status; cover topo diamond+self-dep; examples smoke
description: Low-risk leftovers: (1) pkg/health tests only assert a nil-returning check — a failing check producing 503/degraded status is never asserted. (2) pkg/lakta sortModules diamond deps and self-dependency (ownerIdx==i) skip branches untested (cycle + missing-provider ARE tested). (3) examples/microservices has a build-only CI guard (adequate for API-break) but no runtime smoke test — optional.
acceptance_criteria: health degraded status asserted; topo-sort diamond + self-dep branches covered.
close_reason: Added health degraded/down status tests + topo diamond + self-dependency tests. examples smoke omitted (build-guard adequate). Lint clean.
